about.heroTitle: A simpler way to manage flight disruption claims
about.heroText: Fly Friendly helps travelers organize claim information, upload required documents, and track progress in one clear, step-by-step flow.
about.missionTitle: Make passenger rights easier to use
about.missionText: We turn a complex compensation process into a guided experience. From eligibility checks to document uploads and case tracking, our work is focused on clarity, speed, and trust.
about.missionStatText: Potential compensation for eligible disrupted flights.
about.whatWeDoTitle: Practical support from first check to final update

whatWeDoItems.title: Eligibility Check
whatWeDoItems.text: We help passengers understand whether a delay, cancellation, denied boarding, or missed connection may qualify.

whatWeDoItems.title: Document Flow
whatWeDoItems.text: Travelers can prepare the details and documents needed to support a compensation case.

whatWeDoItems.title: Case Handling
whatWeDoItems.text: Fly Friendly can manage airline communication and keep the claim process organized.

about.valuesTitle: Built around trust, clarity, and action

values.title: Transparency
values.text: Clear steps, practical explanations, and status updates that make the claim process easier to follow.

values.title: Simplicity
values.text: A guided flow that helps travelers move from disruption to submitted claim without unnecessary friction.

values.title: Security
values.text: Careful handling of passenger details, uploaded documents, and case information throughout the process.

values.title: User-first
values.text: Built for real passengers who need practical support after stressful travel disruptions.

home.faqText: Everything you need to know about claiming compensation.

faqs.question: Am I eligible for flight compensation?
faqs.answer: In general, you may be eligible if your flight was delayed by 3+ hours, canceled, or you were denied boarding, and the disruption was within the airline's responsibility.

faqs.question: How much can I receive for my claim?
faqs.answer: Compensation varies by distance, delay length, and applicable regulation. Many cases fall into fixed tiers, for example up to EUR 600 in some jurisdictions.

faqs.question: How long does the compensation process take?
faqs.answer: Timelines vary. Straightforward cases can be resolved in a few weeks, while others may take longer if the airline disputes the claim.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to submit?
faqs.answer: Typically you need basic flight details and proof of travel, such as a booking confirmation, boarding pass, and any delay or cancellation notice.

faqs.question: What if the airline refuses my claim?
faqs.answer: If an airline rejects the claim, we can review the reason, respond with supporting evidence, and escalate when appropriate.

faqs.question: Do I have to deal with the airline myself?
faqs.answer: No. Once you submit your details, we handle communication with the airline and manage the paperwork.

faqs.question: What are your service fees?
faqs.answer: We generally operate on a success-based fee model: if we do not win, you do not pay.

faqs.question: Is my personal data safe?
faqs.answer: We only use your information to process your claim and communicate with relevant parties. Data is shared only when necessary to pursue compensation.

approved.title: Thank you, your claim is on its way!
approved.text: Thanks for trusting Fly Friendly, your request has safely landed in our system. Our flight compensation team is now reviewing your case and will keep you informed as it progresses.
approved.nextText: Every claim is handled by a dedicated team, specialists, negotiators, and legal pros who know air travel inside out.
nextSteps: Our specialists confirm all flight data, from departure records to delay reasons.
AI-assisted tools help us gather the strongest arguments, so airlines can't ignore your rights.
Our negotiation team contacts the airline directly, speeding up responses and ensuring fair compensation.
Sometimes airlines resist. That's when our legal network steps in to take it further, still no fee unless you win.
When your claim is approved, we transfer the money straight to your account.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how Fly Friendly collects, uses, stores, and protects information when you use our flight compensation website, claim flow, contact forms, and newsletter.

sections.title: 1. Who we are
sections.text: Fly Friendly is a flight compensation support service. We help passengers check potential eligibility, submit flight disruption details, upload supporting documents, and track claim progress.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect
sections.text: Depending on how you use the website, we may collect:
items: Contact details such as name, email address, phone number, and message content.
Flight and claim details such as airline, route, dates, delay length, cancellation details, booking reference, and case status.
Documents you choose to upload, such as booking confirmations, boarding passes, airline notices, or identity information if required for a claim.
Technical information such as device type, browser, IP address, pages visited, and cookie identifiers.
Newsletter and marketing preferences if you subscribe to updates.

sections.title: 3. How we use information
items: To assess and manage flight compensation claims.
To communicate with you about your case, support request, or submitted message.
To prepare claim documentation and communicate with airlines or relevant parties when needed.
To improve website performance, user experience, fraud prevention, and security.
To send newsletters or updates only when you have subscribed or otherwise permitted this.

sections.title: 4. Sharing information
sections.text: We may share information with airlines, legal or operational partners, payment providers, analytics providers, hosting providers, and other service providers only when necessary for claim handling, website operation, compliance, or support.

sections.title: 5. Retention and security
sections.text: We keep information only as long as reasonably needed for the purpose it was collected, including claim handling, record keeping, legal obligations, dispute resolution, and fraud prevention. We use reasonable organizational and technical measures to protect personal information.

sections.title: 6. Your rights
sections.text: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to certain processing of your personal information. You may also withdraw marketing consent. To make a request, contact <emailLink>info@fly-friendly.com</emailLink>.

sections.title: 7. Contact
sections.text: Questions about privacy can be sent to <emailLink>info@fly-friendly.com</emailLink>.

privacy.note: This page is a practical website policy draft based on the Fly Friendly service model. It should be reviewed by counsel before production use.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the Fly Friendly website, claim submission tools, referral pages, newsletter forms, and related online services.

sections.title: 1. Use of the website
sections.text: You may use the website to learn about passenger rights, check potential compensation, submit contact requests, and begin a claim. You agree to use the website lawfully and not interfere with its operation, security, or availability.

sections.title: 2. Claim information
sections.text: You are responsible for providing accurate, complete, and up-to-date flight, passenger, contact, and document information. Inaccurate information may delay review or prevent claim handling.

sections.title: 3. No guarantee of compensation
sections.text: Eligibility depends on route, disruption type, delay length, airline responsibility, evidence, applicable law, and other factors. Fly Friendly may help assess and pursue claims, but we do not guarantee that every submitted case will succeed.

sections.title: 4. Fees and payments
sections.text: The website describes a success-based approach such as no win, no fee. Any final fee, commission, or payment arrangement should be confirmed in the claim agreement or service terms presented during the claim process.

sections.title: 5. User conduct
items: Do not submit false, misleading, or fraudulent information.
Do not upload files that contain malware, illegal material, or information you are not authorized to share.
Do not copy, scrape, disrupt, reverse engineer, or misuse the website.

sections.title: 6. Content and intellectual property
sections.text: The Fly Friendly name, website design, text, graphics, and brand assets belong to Fly Friendly or its licensors. You may not reuse them commercially without permission.

sections.title: 7. Limitation of liability
sections.text: The website is provided for general information and service access. To the fullest extent permitted by law, Fly Friendly is not liable for indirect losses, unavailable website access, third-party actions, airline decisions, or consequences of inaccurate information submitted by users.

sections.title: 8. Contact
sections.text: For questions about these terms, contact <emailLink>info@fly-friendly.com</emailLink>.

terms.note: This Terms of Use page is a draft based on the site functionality and should be reviewed by counsel before production use.

cookies.title: Cookies Policy
cookies.intro: This policy explains how Fly Friendly may use cookies and similar technologies to keep the website working, understand usage, improve performance, and support marketing.

sections.title: 1. What cookies are
sections.text: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. Similar technologies include local storage, pixels, tags, and analytics identifiers.

sections.title: 2. Types of cookies we may use
items: Essential cookies: Required for navigation, security, forms, and core website functions.
Preference cookies: Remember choices such as language or display settings.
Analytics cookies: Help us understand page visits, traffic sources, and how users interact with the website.
Marketing cookies: May help measure campaigns, referral activity, or newsletter performance.

sections.title: 3. Why we use cookies
sections.text: Cookies help us keep the site reliable, improve the claim journey, understand which pages are useful, detect technical issues, and measure the performance of content such as referral and newsletter campaigns.

sections.title: 4. Third-party tools
sections.text: We may use third-party providers for analytics, hosting, forms, security, and marketing measurement. These providers may set cookies or collect technical data according to their own policies.

sections.title: 5. Managing cookies
sections.text: You can block, delete, or restrict cookies through your browser settings. Some essential website functions may not work correctly if cookies are disabled.

sections.title: 6. Contact
sections.text: Questions about cookies can be sent to <emailLink>info@fly-friendly.com</emailLink>.

cookies.note: This Cookies Policy is a draft based on the Fly Friendly website model and should be reviewed before production use.
